name: Honda Civic Si (9th Gen)
reliability: 8/10 — the 9th-gen Si swapped to the larger 2.4L K24Z7, the biggest-displacement engine in Si history, trading some top-end scream for a much fatter, more usable midrange (and it kept the helical LSD). The K24 is durable and, being chain-driven, has no timing belt. It launched at 201 hp / 170 lb-ft in 2012–2013 and was retuned to 205 hp / 174 lb-ft for 2014–2015. The main criticisms were the cheap early-2012 interior (fixed in the 2013 refresh) and the same K-series synchro/VTC quirks — not durability.
cost_to_own: Insurance: Moderate-high · Maintenance ~$450/yr · Solid, stable values — the torquey K24 makes it a favorite affordable Si
popular_mods: Bolt-ons (intake, header, cat-back) plus a tune — the K24 makes strong, tractable power
Coilovers + sway bars to work with the factory LSD
A short shifter and fresh bushings for tighter throws
Sticky summer tires to put the midrange torque down
Honest take: the K24's torque makes this the easiest Si to enjoy stock — light mods go a long way

generations.9th Gen FB6 Sedan (2012–2015): The four-door Si — 2.4L K24Z7, 6-speed, helical LSD. Practical, torquey, and the volume seller.
generations.9th Gen FG4 Coupe (2012–2015): The two-door with the same drivetrain for buyers who wanted the sportier body.
generations.2014 retune: Honda revised the exhaust for 2014, bumping output from 201 hp / 170 lb-ft to 205 hp / 174 lb-ft.

common_issues: Cheap-feeling interior on the 2012 cars specifically — Honda improved it in the 2013 refresh (cosmetic, not mechanical)
3rd-gear synchro wear on hard-shifted examples — feel for notchiness
VTC actuator cold-start rattle on the K-series — usually benign
Clutch wear on modified or hard-driven cars
Otherwise very few mechanical complaints — the K24 is stout
maintenance_tips: No timing belt — the K24 is chain-driven, saving a major service
Keep the transmission fluid fresh and shift deliberately to protect the synchros
Address a persistent VTC rattle; maintain a tight oil interval
Prefer a 2013+ car for the improved interior, or a 2014–2015 for the small power bump
Inspect the clutch on any tuned car before purchase
